concerns.text: Task T038 implements a 'one-sample t-test against theoretical bound' and T044/T045 enforce a 'coincidence point' check. The plan.md explicitly revises this methodology to a 'Paired T-Test' (Heuristic vs. Full-Batch) and replaces 'coincidence' with 'correlation' to avoid tautology. The task order enforces a consumer (T038) to run before the producer (the revised statistical logic in the plan) is implemented, creating a semantic dependency violation where the tasks contradict the plan's methodological revision.

Task T044 requires verifying a 'coincidence point' (spec SC-002). The plan.md 'Success Criteria Alignment' section explicitly states SC-002 is revised to measure 'correlation' rather than exact coincidence. T044 attempts to consume a metric (exact coincidence) that the plan has declared invalid, violating the producer-consumer flow where the plan defines the valid output schema.

Task T021 implements 'one-sample t-test' as a primary function in `src/analysis/stats.py`. The plan.md 'Key Methodological Revision' explicitly forbids this approach as a 'tautology' and mandates a 'Paired T-Test' as the primary validation. T021 consumes a methodological requirement (one-sample test) that the plan (producer of the strategy) has rejected, creating a semantic ordering violation.
